widget_structure_guidance.overview: This file defines the expected widget file layout and how to reason about required and optional files while still respecting the actual conventions present in the repository.

element.purpose: Primary HTML structure for the widget
Widget behavior, data access, and event handling
Widget metadata such as binding information, options, events, and referenced business objects
Human-readable explanation of expected input data
Notes about event behavior and event-handling expectations
Per-event scripts when the widget requires specific BAW event handlers
element.details:
Event handler files are OPTIONAL and should only be created when the widget needs them. Available events: load.js, change.js, view.js, validate.js, unload.js
Examples of when to create event handlers: - load.js: Widget needs initialization logic or custom event registration - change.js: Widget needs to respond to data changes and update its display - view.js: Widget needs to refresh when becoming visible - validate.js: Widget needs custom validation logic - unload.js: Widget needs cleanup (timers, listeners, resources)
Many simple widgets don't need any event handlers. Only create what's necessary. The events/ folder should always exist but may be empty.

examples.example: TasksList widget uses load.js and change.js for data rendering
Breadcrumb widget uses only change.js for navigation updates
DateOutput widget has no event handlers (simple display widget)

element.purpose: CSS styles and minimal container structure for BAW Designer preview integration
element.details: Contains embedded CSS styles and a basic HTML container. Preview content is dynamically generated by the companion JavaScript file, not hardcoded in HTML.

element.purpose: BAW Designer preview integration using mixObject pattern
element.details: Implements the mixObject interface required by IBM BAW Designer's preview system. Uses BPMExt-Controls.preview.js layer and Dojo modules to generate preview content that appears in the designer when the widget is placed on a coach view.
element.critical_note: This is NOT a standalone preview file. It MUST integrate with BAW's preview system using the mixObject pattern. See 8_preview_integration.xml for complete requirements.

element.purpose: Business object schema definitions used by widget metadata
Palette icon for widget catalog display (120x120px square SVG)
element.details: A visual representation of the widget shown in the BAW Designer widget catalog. Should be a 120x120px square SVG that clearly illustrates the widget's appearance and purpose using the widget's actual styling and colors.

rule.statement: Match the real folder and file naming used by the target widget or established project pattern
rule.rationale: The repository contains some naming variation, so the mode should preserve local conventions when modifying existing widgets and use the dominant pattern when creating new ones.

rule.statement: Do not invent required files that are not justified by repository usage
rule.rationale: If a file type is not consistently present across widgets, describe it as conditional or recommended instead of universally mandatory.

rule.statement: Keep metadata, preview files, and documentation aligned with the real runtime structure
rule.rationale: Widget projects become fragile when file layout and behavioral descriptions drift apart.

example.name: simple_widget
example.usage: Use as a reference for smaller display-oriented widgets

example.name: event_and_business_object_widget
example.usage: Use as a reference for widgets with config metadata, business objects, and events

example.name: complete_widget_family
example.usage: Use as a reference for a well-documented widget with preview assets and support files
